methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Tempus AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $6B+

private.name: Freenome
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $2.6B

private.name: Alto Neuroscience
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Recursion Pharmaceuticals, a public-stage biotech company, is evaluated against a peer universe of 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, supplemented by 8 private frontier-tech peers including Tempus AI, Freenome, and Alto Neuroscience.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, the median valuation is $2.6B, with a range spanning $529M (Alto Neuroscience) to $6.0B (Tempus AI). This method directly anchors the valuation to observable market pricing for similar-stage biotech companies. The public-comp anchoring method using sector P/S multiples (median 7.4x across 3 tickers: RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) cannot be applied, as Recursion's revenue is not disclosed.

The analysis supports a triangulated valuation range of $2.2B to $3.0B, centered on a $2.6B median. Recursion's current valuation of $1.8B sits below this range. Key uncertainties include the wide spread within the peer set ($529M to $6.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in clinical stage and commercial readiness across biotech peers, and the lack of revenue data to validate multiples-based approaches. A single applicable method limits conviction; broader revenue visibility or expansion of the peer set would strengthen the analysis.
